name: Addis Invoice OCR
description: AI-powered invoice processing pipeline for small businesses in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Users submit invoices (PDF/JPG/PNG) via a React web dashboard, Telegram bot, or WhatsApp. n8n orchestrates Google Document AI for OCR text extraction and DeepSeek for structured data parsing. Extracted data (vendor, date, line items, grand total) is written to Google Sheets and surfaced in a searchable, filterable invoice history with detail modals and PDF export. Multi-tenant filtering via persistent anonymous User_ID ensures each browser sees only its own invoices — no login required.

frontend.name: addis-invoice-dashboard

branding.logo: /Alogo.png — custom logo image used in Navbar, footer, and favicon
branding.icon_note: Replaced the old 'L' text-in-a-box placeholder across all components

pages./: Landing page with hero (video placeholder), how-it-works steps, pricing tiers (Starter 300ETB / Pro 750ETB / Enterprise Custom), testimonials grid, FAQ accordion, and WhatsApp-driven CTA sections — all styled in cyan theme
pages./upload: Drag-and-drop invoice upload with progress bar, processing spinner, and result card with detail modal and View Latest Invoice button
pages./invoices: Searchable, filterable, sortable invoice history table with detail modal, PDF download, and pagination (10 per page)

components: Navbar — sticky top nav with Alogo.png branding, anchor links to landing sections, Upload/Invoices NavLinks, and 'Book a demo' CTA
UploadZone — drag-and-drop file upload area with type/size validation and visual drag-over feedback
ProcessingSpinner — animated spinner shown during OCR+AI extraction
ResultsCard — extracted invoice preview with line items table, summary, and action buttons (Save, Download, View Invoice, Upload Another)
FilterBar — search by vendor/item, date range filters, clear button, and showing-N-of-M count
InvoiceTable — paginated (10/page), sortable table with view, download, and delete actions; skeleton loading rows; empty state
DetailsModal — full-screen overlay showing vendor, date, total, source, line items, items summary, and PDF export

hooks.useInvoiceUpload: Manages upload lifecycle — idle → uploading → processing → uploaded/completed/failed. Posts multipart FormData (file + user_id) to n8n webhook via centralized API client. Handles progress, timeout (150s), CORS, React StrictMode remount safety, and error classification.
hooks.useInvoiceList: Fetches invoice history from n8n get-invoices webhook, passing userId as query param for multi-tenant filtering. Provides search, date filtering, sorting, pagination, and delete (local hide).

lib.api: Centralized Axios instance pointing to VITE_API_URL. Appends webhook paths automatically. Exports getUserId() which generates a persistent anonymous ID (anon_<random>) stored in localStorage — survives refreshes and tab closes. This ID is sent as user_id (upload) and userId (list) for multi-tenant sheet filtering.
lib.multi_tenant: Each browser gets a unique persistent anonymous User_ID on first visit. Both upload and list webhooks receive this ID so the n8n workflow can filter/shard rows per tenant in the master Google Sheet — no login required.

OCR-3.3-web.description: Main invoice processing workflow with three entry points: Web upload, Telegram bot, and WhatsApp. Supports multi-tenant filtering via user_id field.

flow: 2. Generate Job ID — creates job_id, status=queued, passes binary file and user_id
3. Respond to Webhook — immediately returns {"status":"success","message":"..."} with CORS headers
4. Extract from File — Google Document AI OCR text extraction
5. Switch PDF/Image — routes to appropriate HTTP Request node
6. AI Agent + DeepSeek Chat Model + Structured Output Parser — parses vendor, date, items, grand_total from extracted text
7. Edit Fields — normalizes and formats extracted data, includes user_id for tenant filtering
8. If valid invoice → Append or update row in Google Sheets (includes user_id column)
9. If not valid → Send rejection message (Telegram only)
10. Switch → Send Telegram/WhatsApp notification on success
1. Telegram Trigger — listens for file messages
2. Get a file — downloads file from Telegram
3. Extract from File — Google Document AI OCR
4. Switch PDF/Image → HTTP Request to Document AI
5. Converges with web path at AI Agent for parsing and Sheets storage

whatsapp_path.flow: WhatsApp integration added in v3.3 — receives invoice files via WhatsApp and feeds into the shared OCR+AI pipeline

ai_models.ocr: Google Document AI (australia-southeast1 region, processor for PDF/image OCR)
ai_models.parsing: DeepSeek Chat Model — deepseek-v4-flash (via n8n LangChain nodes with Structured Output Parser)

structured_output_schema.is_invoice: boolean — true if valid invoice/receipt, false for unrelated content
structured_output_schema.date: string — invoice date (empty if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.vendor_name: string — standardized vendor name, Amharic translated to English (empty if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.grand_total: number — final total amount due (0 if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.items_summary: string — concise text summary of purchased items (empty if not an invoice)

multi_tenant.user_id_field: Sent from frontend as user_id in FormData (upload) and userId query param (list). Persisted per-row in Google Sheets so the n8n get-invoices webhook can filter rows to the requesting tenant.
multi_tenant.frontend_id_format: anon_<10-char random><timestamp> — generated once per browser, stored in localStorage under key 'addis_invoice_user_id'

immediate_ack.message: File uploaded successfully! Your invoice is being processed in the background.

response_format.async_result: Written to Google Sheets (with user_id column), surfaced via get-invoices webhook filtered by userId

get-invoices.description: GET webhook that reads all rows from Google Sheets, filters by userId query param (multi-tenant), takes the 15 most recent, reverses them (newest first), and returns as a JSON array with normalized field names.
flow: 1. Webhook receives GET request with ?userId= query param
3. Code node — filters rows by userId, slices last 15 rows, reverses, normalizes fields (vendor, date, grand_total, items_summary, source, id)

backend.data_store: Google Sheets — append/update rows via n8n Google Sheets node (Sheet: OCR-dev → Sheet1). Each row includes a user_id column for multi-tenant partitioning.

data_flow.upload: User submits file via Web/Telegram/WhatsApp → Frontend attaches user_id from localStorage → n8n acknowledges immediately → Background: Document AI OCR → DeepSeek AI parsing → Google Sheets (row includes user_id) → Telegram/WhatsApp notification
data_flow.history: Frontend GET /get-invoices?userId=anon_xxx → n8n reads Google Sheets, filters by userId → Returns last 15 rows (newest first) for that tenant → Frontend displays in sortable/filterable table
data_flow.detail_view: User clicks View Invoice → Frontend opens DetailsModal overlay with full invoice data (vendor, date, total, source, line items, summary) and PDF download

channels.web: React dashboard with drag-and-drop upload at /upload, searchable/filterable history at /invoices
channels.telegram: Telegram bot accepting invoice files and returning extracted summary
channels.whatsapp: WhatsApp channel for invoice submission (added in v3.3). Landing page also uses WhatsApp (0988817281) for free-trial signups and enterprise inquiries via pre-filled message links.

pricing.trial: 7-day free trial via WhatsApp — no credit card required

key_fixes_applied: Removed manual Content-Type: multipart/form-data header — let axios set boundary automatically
Fixed React StrictMode double-mount bug — mountedRef not reset to true on remount, silently dropping all upload state callbacks
Added View Invoice button to upload result screen — fetches latest invoice from API and opens DetailsModal
Centralized API configuration in lib/api.ts with VITE_API_URL env var and localhost fallback
Separated dev/prod environments with single VITE_API_URL in .env files
Redesigned homepage with landing page sections (hero, how-it-works, pricing, testimonials, FAQ, footer CTA) and cyan color theme
Rebranded all Ledger references to Addis Invoice
Replaced text-based 'L' icon with Alogo.png custom logo across Navbar, footer, and favicon
Implemented multi-tenant filtering via persistent anonymous User_ID (localStorage) passed to n8n webhooks — each browser sees only its own invoices with no login required
Switched webhook paths from named routes to UUID-based paths for both upload and get-invoices endpoints
target_users: Small businesses in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Supports Amharic and English invoice formats. Multi-tenant architecture allows shared Google Sheet with per-browser data isolation.

deployment.frontend: Vercel (build: npm run build, output: dist/)
